what_it_does: Implements an HTTP TTS service for Canopy Labs' Orpheus model on port 4125. It loads a vLLM-backed OrpheusModel, patches its engine setup, runs a persistent asyncio event loop to stream LLM token text, decodes the tokens through the SNAC codec into 16-bit mono PCM WAV audio, and exposes synth and voices endpoints via voice.engines._service.serve.
observations: Reads the tts-orpheus config block from ops/services.json as the authoritative source for model, max_model_len, and gpu_util, falling back to COMPANY_ORPHEUS_* env vars.
Prepends the interpreter's bin directory to PATH so Triton can find ninja, and disables the FlashInfer sampler via VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0 before any vLLM import.

Uses the cached model canopylabs/orpheus-3b-0.1-ft by default to avoid a large download, and monkey-patches OrpheusModel._setup_engine because the upstream constructor only accepts model_name and dtype.
Defaults to attention_backend FLASH_ATTN, eager graphs off, gpu_memory_utilization 0.6, and max_model_len 8192, all overridable via env vars.

Runs a single persistent asyncio event loop in a daemon thread and submits every synthesis request via run_coroutine_threadsafe to avoid per-call asyncio.run closing the loop and hanging later requests.
Drives m.engine.generate directly with custom SamplingParams and a unique request_id, queues token texts, and feeds them to orpheus_tts.decoder.tokens_decoder_sync.
Writes the decoded PCM chunks into an in-memory mono 16-bit 24kHz WAV and raises an error if no audio was produced.
Installs a SIGTERM handler that calls sys.exit(0) so vLLM's EngineCore process is cleaned up instead of becoming an orphan.
standouts: The persistent event-loop fix for sequential synthesis hangs.
The layered environment tuning for attention and sampler backends plus PATH setup.
Registry-driven config as the single source of truth for engine launch parameters.
The monkey-patch of OrpheusModel._setup_engine to inject custom AsyncEngineArgs.
conventions: Uses module-level singletons for the loaded model and the event loop.
Monkey-patches a third-party class method to adapt its internals.
Reads registry config with stdlib json so it can run in an isolated venv without company modules.
Accepts a speed argument in synth per the shared contract but does not apply it.
Documents root-cause fixes with inline comments including dates and issue references.
concerns: Monkey-patching OrpheusModel._setup_engine is brittle to upstream changes.
Global mutable state for _model and _loop with no explicit shutdown or cleanup path.
Opens the registry JSON without an explicit close context manager.
The speed parameter is silently ignored.
Exceptions from the producer are re-raised in the consumer but may lose original traceback context.
Hard-coded voice bank and sample rate.
notes: A battle-hardened vLLM/Orpheus TTS service launcher that aligns launch parameters with registry config and works around upstream loop, attention, and process-lifecycle issues.
questions: Which exact versions of orpheus-speech and vllm are installed, and is the constructor drift still present?
How is ops/services.json kept synchronized with deployed engine parameters?
How does the persistent loop behave under concurrent synthesis requests?
How is the isolated orpheus venv provisioned and why are company modules unavailable there?
What happens if the registry file is missing or malformed beyond returning an empty config?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to run Orpheus-TTS as an HTTP service on :4125; the file does exactly that while adding config loading, environment fixes, engine patching, and a persistent loop to keep sequential requests from hanging.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make a stock Orpheus/vLLM setup deployable and stable in this company's environment by aligning config, patching around upstream API drift, and eliminating per-call asyncio loop churn that caused hangs.
novelty: The combination of registry config as the single source of truth for engine launch parameters, a persistent event-loop fix, and layered attention/sampler environment tuning is distinct from a plain OrpheusModel wrapper.
contribution: It contributes a working, config-aligned Orpheus-TTS HTTP service with concrete fixes for sequential hangs, vLLM EngineCore orphaning, and attention/sampler backend selection, wrapped in a synth/voices interface compatible with the shared engine service contract.
summary_for_embedding: company/voice/engines/orpheus.py is an HTTP TTS service wrapper for the Orpheus-TTS vLLM model that loads a cached model, patches engine setup, streams tokens on a persistent asyncio loop, decodes them to mono 16-bit PCM WAV, and exposes synth/voices endpoints.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to deploy Orpheus-TTS reliably as a company voice engine by eliminating sequential-request hangs, aligning launch parameters with the registry config, and working around upstream vLLM/Orpheus environment and API issues.

symbols.does: Reads the tts-orpheus config block from ops/services.json using stdlib json, returning an empty dict on any error.
symbols.description: It locates the project root relative to this file, opens ops/services.json, and extracts services.tts-orpheus.config. It is intentionally stdlib-only because this module runs in an isolated venv without company modules.

symbols.does: Lazy-loads and caches the OrpheusModel, monkey-patching its _setup_engine to inject custom AsyncEngineArgs.
symbols.description: It imports orpheus_tts and vllm, defines a replacement _setup_engine method that builds AsyncEngineArgs with the registry or env-derived model, gpu utilization, max model length, eager flag, and attention backend, assigns it to OrpheusModel._setup_engine, then instantiates the model once and caches it in _model.

symbols.does: Replacement for OrpheusModel._setup_engine that constructs AsyncEngineArgs and returns an AsyncLLMEngine.
symbols.description: It hard-codes the engine configuration derived from registry or env variables, including the cached model name, dtype from the OrpheusModel instance, enforce_eager, gpu_memory_utilization, max_model_len, and attention_backend, then creates the engine via AsyncLLMEngine.from_engine_args.

symbols.does: Creates and starts a single daemon-thread event loop for the process lifetime.
symbols.description: Under a lock, it creates a new asyncio event loop and starts it with run_forever in a daemon thread named orpheus-engine-loop, returning the loop so all synthesis requests can be submitted to it.

symbols.does: Yields token-text strings for one synthesis request by running generation on the persistent loop.
symbols.description: It formats the prompt via the OrpheusModel, builds SamplingParams matching the stock wrapper, generates a unique request id, and schedules an async producer on the persistent loop that feeds token texts into a thread-safe queue; the generator yields each queued text until a None sentinel ends the stream.

symbols.does: Async coroutine that drives m.engine.generate and enqueues output texts or an exception.
symbols.description: It iterates over the vLLM engine's async generate results for the formatted prompt and sampling params, puts each output text onto a queue, catches any exception and puts it on the queue, then enqueues None to signal completion.

symbols.does: Validates the voice and returns synthesized WAV bytes for the given text.
symbols.description: It selects the voice or default, verifies it against VOICE_BANK, ensures the engine is loaded, runs the token-text generator through orpheus_tts.decoder.tokens_decoder_sync to obtain PCM chunks, writes them into an in-memory mono 16-bit 24kHz WAV, and raises an error if no audio was produced. The speed argument is accepted but not applied.

symbols.does: Returns the list of supported Orpheus voices and the default voice.
symbols.description: It returns a tuple containing a copy of VOICE_BANK and DEFAULT_VOICE for the shared service contract.
